explanation: The Sigma rule relies on an explicit blocklist of directories in the 'selection_dir' condition. By placing and executing the target file from a directory omitted from this list, such as 'C:\ProgramData\', the command bypasses the detection logic because 'all of selection_*' is no longer satisfied.

The rule author explicitly commented out '\Desktop\' and '\Downloads\' in the 'selection_dir' list to likely reduce false positives. Executing the payload from a user's Desktop directory completely evades the directory string matching.

Depending on the specific SIEM and logging configuration (e.g., if command-line arguments are logged before environment variables are expanded), using the '%TEMP%' environment variable instead of the explicit '\AppData\Local\Temp\' string can bypass the literal string matching defined in the rule.
